rationale: The cited paper appears conceptually central rather than incidental: it studies sequential individual decisions following jointly relevant outcomes in dyads, and the citing paper likewise focuses on how an initial other-directed choice (for a child) shifts a subsequent self-choice under possible sharing. The citing note explicitly positions its contribution as refining sequential-choice understanding in mixed self/other contexts, making this citation a plausible theory/background anchor (and it is directly matched by DOI).
